meta.title: OpenE2EE — Transparency in Digital Communication
meta.description: Real-time auditing of RCS 3.0 and E2EE protocols. Prove that your communications are truly end-to-end encrypted.

hero.title: The era of transparency in digital communication.
hero.subtitle: Real-time auditing of RCS 3.0 and E2EE protocols. Prove that your communications are truly end-to-end encrypted.
hero.intro: Communication platforms claim to be secure, but are they really? OpenE2EE verifies encryption integrity in your messaging traffic through passive network analysis methods.

why.label: Problem & Solution
why.title: Why OpenE2EE?
why.desc: Independently verify the security promises of messaging applications.

problem.title: Problem: Black Box
problem.text: Messaging applications are a 'black box'. You cannot tell whether encryption has been compromised or whether packets are being decrypted at intermediary servers.

solution.title: Solution: Passive Observer
solution.text: OpenE2EE audits packet header analysis, TLS handshake processes, and encryption entropy as a passive observer. It doesn't read your data — it only verifies.
solution.code:
// OpenE2EE analysis engine
tls.fingerprint()  → protocol consistency ✓
header.inspect()   → metadata audit      ✓
entropy.measure()  → encryption strength  ✓

steps.title: TLS Fingerprinting
steps.text: We verify protocol consistency by analyzing client and server fingerprints during the TLS handshake process. JA3/JA3S hashes are compared against known secure profiles.

steps.title: Header & Metadata Audit
steps.text: We inspect packet headers and metadata to verify whether encryption layers are properly implemented.

steps.title: Protocol Validation
steps.text: We evaluate encryption strength through RCS/MLS compatibility checks and entropy measurement. Shannon entropy values prove that encryption is genuinely applied.

matrix.label: Comparison
matrix.title: Transparency matrix
matrix.desc: Protocol security assessment of popular messaging applications.

news.label: Updates
news.title: Latest developments
news.desc: The most recent news and analyses in digital communication security.

articles.title: Apple Launches RCS E2EE Support with iOS 26.5
articles.summary: Apple enabled MLS-based end-to-end encryption under RCS Universal Profile 3.0 in the iOS 26.5 beta. Encrypted RCS messaging between Android and iPhone is now possible.

articles.title: GSMA Announces RCS Universal Profile 3.0
articles.summary: GSMA officially released the RCS 3.0 specification integrating the IETF-standard MLS protocol. This update provides forward secrecy and post-compromise security across the industry.

articles.title: WhatsApp Encryption Debates Continue
articles.summary: Lawsuits over WhatsApp's E2EE promises continue. While Meta defends content security with the Signal protocol, metadata collection practices are being criticized.

articles.title: Telegram MTProto: Default Encryption Deficiency
articles.summary: Security researchers reiterated that Telegram does not use E2EE in standard chats and only provides it in 'Secret Chat' mode.

articles.title: IETF MLS Protocol Rises as Industry Standard
articles.summary: Messaging Layer Security (MLS) is being widely adopted as the IETF standard providing forward secrecy and post-compromise security for both one-to-one and group messaging.

articles.title: The E2EE Metadata Problem: Content is Safe, But What About Metadata?
articles.summary: While E2EE protects message content, metadata such as who talks to whom and when can still be collected, creating a significant privacy gap for users.
